task_completion.description: Extract the first genre from the pipe-delimited genres column as Primary_Genre
task_completion.visual_evidence: Power Query Editor or Data view showing a new or modified column named 'Primary_Genre' containing single genres (e.g., Action, Comedy, Drama) with no pipe '|' characters.

task_completion.description: Filter out null values from gross and budget columns
task_completion.visual_evidence: Power Query Editor showing filter icons applied to the 'gross' and 'budget' columns, or the Filter pane in the Report view showing that blanks/nulls are excluded for these fields.
Power Query Editor, Data view, or Fields pane showing a 'Decade' column or bin group with 10-year grouped values (e.g., 1980, 1990, 2000) derived from the 'title_year' column.

task_completion.description: Create a Matrix visual displaying average gross by Primary_Genre and Decade
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Matrix visual on the Power BI report canvas. The Visualizations pane must show 'Primary_Genre' in the Rows field, 'Decade' in the Columns field, and 'gross' (set to Average) in the Values field.

task_completion.description: Create a Scatter Chart plotting gross against budget categorized by Primary_Genre
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Scatter Chart on the Power BI report canvas. The Visualizations pane must show 'budget' on the X-axis, 'gross' on the Y-axis, and 'Primary_Genre' in the Legend field.

task_completion.description: Export the Matrix visual data to genre_stats.csv
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Export data' menu open on the Matrix visual, or a Save dialog/File Explorer window showing 'genre_stats.csv' being saved to 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\TaskData\'.

task_completion.description: Save the final Power BI report as IMDB_Analysis.pbix
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Power BI application window title displaying 'IMDB_Analysis - Power BI Desktop' or a File Explorer window showing 'IMDB_Analysis.pbix' in 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\TaskData\'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Power BI Desktop GUI to build the report
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the Power BI Desktop interface (canvas, visualizations pane, Power Query Editor). The agent should not use a terminal or Python script to generate a dummy .pbix file.

integrity.description: Verify data transformations were done within Power BI
integrity.visual_evidence: The splitting of genres, filtering of nulls, and binning of decades should be visible as applied steps in Power Query or as DAX formulas, rather than the agent running an external script to pre-process the CSV before loading it into Power BI.

integrity.description: Verify the CSV was exported directly from the Matrix visual
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should click the '...' on the Matrix visual and select 'Export data'. The CSV should not be generated by a separate Python script or manually typed.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset initially contains 5043 rows. After filtering null 'gross' and 'budget' values, 3891 rows remain. The 'genres' column uses a pipe ('|') delimiter; extracting the first genre yields 17 unique primary genres (e.g., Comedy, Action, Drama) with no pipe characters. The 'title_year' spans 1916-2016, which should result in Decade bins from 1920 to 2010. The Matrix visual must aggregate 'gross' using the Average function (not Sum). The Scatter Chart must have 'budget' on the X-axis and 'gross' on the Y-axis.
